title: Amarnath, Vaishno Devi yatras halted amid heavy rain alert - MSN
article_text:
Weather halts pilgrimages: IMD forecasts heavy rain, flash floods and landslides have prompted J&K to suspend Amarnath, Vaishno Devi and Shiv Khori yatras from July 19.
Routes and camps shut: No onward movement is allowed from Baltal, Pahalgam, Nunwan-Chandanwari or Bhagwati Nagar base camps until weather and route safety are reassessed.
Record pilgrim turnout: Despite the halt, more than 3.76 lakh devotees have already visited the Amarnath cave shrine since the yatra began on July 3.

title: Island Monsoon Cycling Tour – 2026 Successfully Organised in the City - Andaman Chronicle
article_text:
Island Monsoon Cycling Tour – 2026 Successfully Organised in the City
By Denis Giles / July 19, 2026

Sri Vijaya Puram, July 19: The Department of Sports and Youth Affairs, A&N Administration in collaboration with Department of Tourism, A&N Administration and Sports Authority of India (SAI) has organised the Island Monsoon Cycling Tour–2026 today to promote fitness, healthy living, eco-friendly transportation, and adventure tourism in the Islands.

The cycling tour commenced in the morning from Netaji Stadium and concluded at Corbyn’s Cove, witnessing enthusiastic participation from cyclists of all age groups.

Smti. Shweta K. Sugathan, IPS, SP (SA), as the chief guest flagged off the cycling tour in the presence of the Director (Sports) and commended the Department of Sports and Youth Affairs for organizing the event.

The Island Monsoon Cycling Tour–2026 concluded, reaffirming the Administration’s commitment to promoting sports, fitness, active lifestyles, and tourism through community-based sporting events across the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. The overwhelming response from the participants reflected the growing enthusiasm for cycling and recreational sports in the Union Territory.

title: IMD forecasts widespread rain across north India, warns of extremely heavy rainfall in hill states - The Kashmir Horizon
article_text:
Panchkula July 20 (UNI) The India Meteorological Department (IMD), in its morning bulletin issued at 0809 hours IST on Monday, forecast fairly widespread to widespread rainfall across large parts of northwest, central and east India over the next week, with isolated extremely heavy rainfall warned for Uttarakhand, Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh in the initial days of the spell.



According to the IMD, heavy to very heavy rainfall at a few places, with extremely heavy rainfall at isolated places, is likely over Uttarakhand on July 20; over Jammu and Kashmir during July 20-22; and over Himachal Pradesh on July 20 and 21.



The department cautioned that the red-colour warning used for such alerts does not denote a “red alert” but signals that authorities and residents should “take action.”



The weather office said fairly widespread to widespread rainfall is likely over Himachal Pradesh, Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad and Uttarakhand during July 20-26. Isolated thunderstorm, lightning and gusty winds reaching 40-50 kmph, gusting to 60 kmph, are also forecast over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad during July 20-23, while isolated thunderstorm and lightning are likely over Uttarakhand through the week and over Uttar Pradesh during July 20-22.



For Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi and Punjab, the IMD forecast fairly widespread to widespread rainfall during July 20-23, tapering to isolated to scattered rainfall during July 24-26.



Uttar Pradesh is expected to receive fairly widespread to widespread rainfall during July 20-24, with isolated to scattered rain on July 25 and 26. East Rajasthan is likely to see fairly widespread to widespread rainfall during July 23-26, while West Rajasthan will see isolated to scattered rainfall through the week, and East Rajasthan isolated to scattered rain during July 20-22.



The bulletin also flagged isolated heavy rainfall over several northwestern states between July 20 and 26, including Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi and Punjab on July 23; Uttar Pradesh during July 23-26; West Rajasthan during July 21-24; and East Rajasthan through the week.

Isolated very heavy rainfall is additionally likely over Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Punjab and Uttar Pradesh during July 20-22, apart from Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad on July 23, Himachal Pradesh on July 22 and Uttarakhand on July 21. Isolated thunderstorm, lightning and gusty winds of 30-40 kmph, gusting to 50 kmph, are forecast over Rajasthan throughout the week.



In central India, the IMD said isolated to scattered rainfall is likely over West Madhya Pradesh during July 20-21, and over Vidarbha during July 20-21 and again on July 25-26. This will give way to fairly widespread to widespread rainfall over West Madhya Pradesh during July 22-26, over Chhattisgarh and East Madhya Pradesh through the week, and over Vidarbha during July 22-24. Isolated thunderstorm, lightning and gusty winds of 30-40 kmph, gusting to 50 kmph, are forecast over Madhya Pradesh during July 20-23, along with isolated thunderstorm and lightning over Chhattisgarh and Vidarbha during the same period. Isolated heavy rainfall is likely over West Madhya Pradesh on July 20 and during July 22-23, over East Madhya Pradesh during July 20-23, over Vidarbha during July 22-23 and over Chhattisgarh during July 20-23.



For east India, the department forecast isolated to scattered rainfall over the Andaman and Nicobar Islands and Odisha through the week, and over Bihar during July 22-26. Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall is likely over Gangetic West Bengal, Jharkhand, and Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim during July 20-26, and over Bihar during July 20-21. Isolated thunderstorm, lightning and gusty winds of 40-50 kmph, gusting to 60 kmph, are likely over Odisha on July 20, with gusty winds of 30-40 kmph, gusting to 50 kmph, over the Andaman and Nicobar Islands during July 20-24 and over Bihar through the week. Isolated thunderstorm and lightning are forecast over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim during the week.



The IMD also warned of isolated heavy rainfall over West Bengal and Sikkim and Gangetic West Bengal during July 21-22; Jharkhand during July 20-21; Bihar during July 21-24; and Odisha during July 20-22, with isolated very heavy rainfall likely over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim, and over Bihar, on July 20.

The forecast and warning for any given day remain valid from 0830 hours IST of that day until 0830 hours IST of the following day, the department said.

title: Heavy rain likely in North Odisha today, yellow warning issued for 6 districts - ODISHA RAY
article_text:
Bhubaneswar : North Odisha is likely to witness heavy rainfall today, the Regional Meteorological Centre has forecast.

Travel Guides & Travelogues

Discover more Odisha Tourism Packages Alumina Refinery Tours Local News

Yellow warning has been issued for 6 districts – Sambalpur, Deogarh, Sundargarh, Keonjhar, Mayurbhanj and Balasore.

One or two places in these districts may receive heavy rainfall ranging between 7 to 11 cm.

Due to the weather system, light to moderate rain is also expected in other parts of the state. Authorities have advised people to remain cautious in view of possible waterlogging and rise in river water levels.

title: Heavy rain alert for few districts of Odisha, warning issued for Puri Rath Yatra - KalingaTV
article_text:
Heavy rain alert for few districts of Odisha, warning issued for Puri Rath Yatra
The IMD has issued a heavy rain alert for several Odisha districts and a special weather advisory for Puri during Rath Yatra. Odisha has also recorded 14% excess rainfall this monsoon.
By KalingaTV Bureau Last updated Jul 20, 2026
(Image: Representational)
 Share

Bhubaneswar: Monsoon stays active in Odisha and the India Meteorological Department (IMD) have issued an alert for heavy rainfall for the districts of Sundaragada, Jharsuguda, Baragada, Sambalpur, Kendujhara, Mayurbhanj, Kandhamala, Kalahandi, Koraput and Malkangiri where an average 7-11cm rainfall have been predicted. OdishaNews Portal

A weather warning alert have been also released for Puri, where the temperature will hover between 32C and 34C, and warned the participants of the Rath Yatra to take shelter in a safe place if they find a thunderstorm and lightning.

As per reports, Bhapur of Nayagarh received maximum rainfall of 98.3mm, followed by Golamunda 84mm, Padampur 82mm and Paikmal 78mm.

Odisha received total of 463.8mm rainfall between June 1 and July 19, which is 14 percent above the average.

title: Heavy rain triggers flash flood at Tuensang fishery farm - Eastern Mirror
article_text:
Floodwaters surge through Thimlak Fishery Farm in Tuensang district on Sunday. (EM Images) Local news app





TUENSANG — Persistent rainfall triggered flash flooding at Thimlak Fishery Farm in Tuensang district at around 11 am on Sunday, causing extensive damage to paddy fields, fisheries and residential structures.





Thimlak, a medium-sized village under Tuensang Sadar Circle, is located about 18 km from Tuensang town along the Tuensang–Mon road.





According to sources, the flood destroyed around 25 paddy fields and fish ponds, besides damaging several huts and concrete houses.





The villagers were engaged in efforts to prevent further damage at the time of filing this report. The full extent of the losses is yet to be assessed, and further details are awaited.

title: ভারী বৃষ্টিতে দেশের ৮ জেলায় বন্যার আশঙ্কা - Sarabangla
article_text:
বন্যার প্রতীকী ছবি। ছবি: সংগৃহীত
ঢাকা: ভারী ও অতি ভারী বৃষ্টিপাত এবং উজানের ঢলের কারণে দেশের আটটি জেলায় স্বল্পমেয়াদি বন্যা পরিস্থিতির সৃষ্টি হতে পারে বলে জানিয়েছে বন্যা পূর্বাভাস ও সতর্কীকরণ কেন্দ্র। আগামী ২৪ থেকে ৭২ ঘণ্টার মধ্যে এই পরিস্থিতির অবনতি ঘটতে পারে বলে আশঙ্কা করা হচ্ছে।
আবহাওয়া সংস্থাগুলোর তথ্য অনুযায়ী, রংপুর ও সিলেট বিভাগসহ ভারতের আসাম, মেঘালয় ও পশ্চিমবঙ্গে ভারী বর্ষণের পূর্বাভাস রয়েছে। এর ফলে দেশের প্রধান নদ-নদীগুলোর পানি বৃদ্ধি পেয়ে বিপৎসীমা অতিক্রম করতে পারে।
বিশেষ করে লালমনিরহাট, নীলফামারী, রংপুর, গাইবান্ধা, কুড়িগ্রাম, সিলেট, সুনামগঞ্জ এবং নেত্রকোনা জেলার নদী-সংলগ্ন নিম্নাঞ্চল প্লাবিত হওয়ার ঝুঁকি রয়েছে। এরইমধ্যে কুশিয়ারা নদীর পানি সিলেটের ফেঞ্চুগঞ্জ ও সুনামগঞ্জের মারকুলি স্টেশনে বিপৎসীমার ওপর দিয়ে প্রবাহিত হচ্ছে।
এ ছাড়া, ব্রহ্মপুত্র-যমুনা নদ-নদীর পানি বৃদ্ধি পেয়ে কুড়িগ্রাম, গাইবান্ধা, জামালপুর, বগুড়া, সিরাজগঞ্জ ও টাঙ্গাইল জেলার কিছু স্থানে সতর্কসীমায় প্রবাহিত হতে পারে। সংশ্লিষ্ট নদ-নদীগুলোর অববাহিকার বাসিন্দাদের ও নিম্নাঞ্চলের মানুষজনকে সতর্ক থাকার পরামর্শ দিয়েছে কর্তৃপক্ষ।
